# Test cases for cmd/crcdiff. Format per case:
#   <command-line args>
#   ----
#   <expected combined stdout+stderr (and any "crcdiff: <error>")>
# Cases are separated by blank lines; '#' lines are comments.
#
# CRC vectors below are over a 1000-byte stream data[i]=byte(i*7+3) unless noted:
#   CRC32C   : single-bit flip of byte 123 bit 4
#   CRC64NVME: single-bit flip of byte 900 bit 2 (100 bytes from end)
#   CRC64ECMA: 2-byte burst (0x05,0x80) at bytes 10-11

# --- CRC32C single bit, length known ---
-length 1000 dd2edff7 b1b11dc1
----
crc32: single bit flip at offset 123, bit 4, mask=0x10 (false positive probability 1 in 130151)

# --- same, 0x prefixes accepted ---
-length 1000 0xdd2edff7 0xb1b11dc1
----
crc32: single bit flip at offset 123, bit 4, mask=0x10 (false positive probability 1 in 130151)

# --- CRC64/NVME default, single bit ---
-length 1000 387e868bd14debed 474f78c3e9de5763
----
crc64: single bit flip at offset 900, bit 2, mask=0x04 (false positive probability 1 in 283796062672455)

# --- CRC64/ECMA, multi-bit burst ---
-length 1000 -crc64 ECMA f033761aeb8e0b26 b25908d6d19f996b
----
crc64: 3 bits flipped across 2 byte(s) at offset 10, mask=0x0580 (false positive probability 1 in 421688057463)

# --- CRC32 IEEE poly, 50-byte stream, byte 7 bit 0 ---
-length 50 -crc32 IEEE b50c79ff f21f8f04
----
crc32: single bit flip at offset 7, bit 0, mask=0x01 (false positive probability 1 in 2603010)

# --- identical CRCs ---
-length 1000 dd2edff7 dd2edff7
----
the two CRCs are identical: no difference

# --- no plausible finding (unrelated 32-bit CRCs) ---
-length 1000 dd2edff7 12345678
----
no plausible finding

# --- no length: warning on stderr, end-relative offset (64-bit) ---
387e868bd14debed 474f78c3e9de5763
----
warning: -length not specified; pass it for an accurate false-positive probability (the probability grows with length). Searched up to 1073741824 bytes; offsets are relative to the end of the stream.
crc64: single bit flip at offset -100, bit 2, mask=0x04 (false positive probability 1 in 264305680)

# --- no length: 32-bit, end-relative offset ---
dd2edff7 b1b11dc1
----
warning: -length not specified; pass it for an accurate false-positive probability (the probability grows with length). Searched up to 524288 bytes; offsets are relative to the end of the stream.
crc32: single bit flip at offset -877, bit 4, mask=0x10 (false positive probability 1 in 248)

# --- error: mismatched widths ---
-length 1000 dd2edff7 474f78c3e9de5763
----
crcdiff: the two CRCs have different widths (32-bit vs 64-bit)

# --- error: bad hex digit count ---
-length 1000 0xZZZ 1234
----
crcdiff: CRC "0xZZZ" must have 8 or 16 hex digits (got 3)

# --- error: too few CRC values ---
-length 1000 dd2edff7
----
crcdiff: expected two CRC values, got 1 (run with -h for usage)

# --- error: no args ---
----
crcdiff: expected two CRC values, got 0 (run with -h for usage)

# --- error: non-positive length ---
-length 0 dd2edff7 b1b11dc1
----
crcdiff: -length must be positive

# --- error: -crc64 with 32-bit CRCs ---
-crc64 ECMA -length 1000 dd2edff7 b1b11dc1
----
crcdiff: -crc64 cannot be used with 32-bit CRCs

# --- error: -crc32 with 64-bit CRCs ---
-crc32 C 387e868bd14debed 474f78c3e9de5763
----
crcdiff: -crc32 cannot be used with 64-bit CRCs

# --- error: both poly flags ---
-crc32 C -crc64 ECMA dd2edff7 b1b11dc1
----
crcdiff: cannot specify both -crc32 and -crc64

# --- error: unknown polynomial ---
-crc32 BOGUS dd2edff7 b1b11dc1
----
crcdiff: unknown crc32 polynomial "BOGUS" (want IEEE or C)
